bugs.title: Trip title not updated after major destination change
bugs.description: After cutting Venice and replacing it with Cinque Terre (message 7), the trip title remained 'First-Time Italy: Rome, Florence, Venice'. The AI did not call UpdateTrip to reflect the new destinations in the title. This is cosmetic but could confuse the user when viewing their trip list.
bugs.expected: Trip title updated to reflect Rome, Florence, Cinque Terre after Venice was removed
bugs.actual: Trip title remained 'First-Time Italy: Rome, Florence, Venice' through completion

ux_issues.description: Free tier daily message limit (10) is too low for a rapid-fire planning persona. Marco's 10-message conversation flow plus the initial selection message exhausted the limit, making companion mode testing impossible. A user who iterates rapidly on plans would hit the wall quickly.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider a higher free-tier limit (15-20) or don't count selection-mode messages against the planning budget.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI created new itinerary items via create_itinerary_items during the final summary (message 10), even though itinerary was already complete. This is redundant and could cause duplicate items if parsing differs.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Message 10 asked for a 'complete final itinerary with all changes'. The AI called create_itinerary_items again, potentially duplicating existing items rather than just summarizing what was already in the itinerary.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI handled 10 rapid-fire planning messages excellently, maintaining context across all turns. Vegetarian preference from message 3 was consistently reflected in all subsequent food recommendations (28 vegetarian references in the final summary). The critical Venice-to-Cinque-Terre swap (message 7) was executed perfectly — delete_itinerary_items removed Venice items and create_itinerary_items added Cinque Terre items. Pompeii day trip and wine tasting additions were both preserved through all modifications. The train pass recommendation (point-to-point over Eurail) was substantive and correct. The only issues were cosmetic: the trip title was never updated to reflect the destination change, and the AI re-created itinerary items unnecessarily in the final summary. Companion mode could not be tested due to hitting the daily message limit. Overall, this is a genuinely useful rapid-iteration planning experience.
